finding: No command reports how much disk the backups directory uses. Users accumulate large backups (real case: 7.3 GB of backups incl. many opencode-db-cleanup-* dirs each holding a full ~2.8 GB DB copy) with no visibility; --clean-backups prunes but never shows current usage. db_show_info shows DB family + session-diff totals but omits backups.
disposition: Add a 'Backups (Disk Storage)' section to `ocman info`: recursively sum default_backup_dir (zip files + opencode-db-cleanup-* dirs), show total size, file/dir count, and oldest/newest. Optionally note prune command.

finding: No per-project disk breakdown exists. Session-diff files (<session_id>.json) CAN be attributed to a project via session.project_id, giving exact per-project on-disk-file bytes. Currently only a global session-diff total is shown.
evidence: ocman.py:6600-6611 (global diff total only); session table project_id
proposed_action: Add an optional per-project breakdown (e.g. `ocman info --by-project` or a `ocman disk` view): for each project, exact session-diff bytes on disk + session/message/token counts, sorted by size.

area: per-project DB bytes are not measurable
finding: Users may expect 'how many MB does project X's DB data use', but SQLite is a single shared file; per-row byte attribution is not directly available and any figure would be an estimate. Presenting estimated per-project DB bytes as if exact would be misleading (honest-docs principle).
evidence: single opencode.db file; no per-project byte accounting
proposed_action: Do NOT report per-project DB *bytes*. Instead report exact per-project row/message/token counts (proxy for DB footprint) and clearly label session-diff bytes as the only exact per-project on-disk figure. If a DB-share estimate is ever wanted, gate it behind an explicit '(estimated)' label.

finding: If a per-project/backups disk view is added, a novice needs to find it. `ocman info` is the natural home (already the 'system information' command); a separate `ocman disk` alias could aid discovery via preprocess_argv.
proposed_action: Surface the new info in `ocman info` by default (backups) and document the per-project flag/subcommand in README + --help; optionally add a 'disk'/'du' natural-language alias.

finding: The TUI Database Admin tab shows DB family details; it does not show backup usage or per-project disk. For parity, the same figures could surface there.
proposed_action: After the CLI feature lands, optionally surface backup total (and per-project view) in the TUI Database Admin tab. Lower priority; CLI first.
